repositories.brief_summary: A landmark week merging the long-awaited "keep expansion" typechecker fix and primitive aliases, alongside major runtime and stdlib improvements.
repositories.new_features_summary: Primitive aliases (RFC #44), Atomic.update, Format.pp_print_iarray, and pp_print_dynarray were merged this week.
repositories.new_features:
- **Primitive aliases** (RFC #44) landed via [#13377](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13377), allowing `external foo = Bar.baz` syntax to alias C primitives; contributed by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke), reviewed by [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop), [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25), and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
- **`Atomic.update`** was merged via [#10798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10798), adding a retry-loop helper `('a -> 'a) -> 'a t -> unit` to `Atomic`, `Atomic.Loc`, and `Atomic.Array`; contributed by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) with extensive review.
- **`Format.pp_print_iarray`** was added via [#14724](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14724) by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), addressing the stdlib oversight noted in [#14320](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14320).
- **`Format.pp_print_dynarray`** was added via [#14726](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14726) by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), building on the iarray printer.
- **ocamltest `cold` predicate** was introduced via [#14713](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14713) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), allowing tests to be disabled by default and run only when explicitly requested, closing [#12418](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12418).
repositories.activity_summary: Major merges this week include the landmark keep-expansion typechecker fix, primitive aliases, runtime domain and ephemeron improvements, and several stdlib additions.
repositories.activity:
- **Keep expansion** typechecker overhaul [#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) was merged after years of development by [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) and [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), fixing long-standing non-termination bugs ([#9314](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/9314), [#13230](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13230), [#13881](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13881), [#14036](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14036)) and principality regressions.
- **Domain unique_id assignment** was moved earlier during domain creation via [#14716](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14716) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), improving debuggability of domain lifecycle logging.
- **Ephemeron orphaning bug fix** for 5.5 was submitted via [#14718](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14718) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and merged, addressing the runtime assertion failures reported in [#14349](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14349).
- **Compaction corner case test** was improved via [#14685](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14685) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), reducing test runtime from 14s to 1.5s while making the test actually reproduce the original bug.
- **GADT scope-escape hint** was added to the typechecker error message via [#14695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14695) by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), providing actionable guidance for users encountering ambivalent type errors.
- **Minor cleanup in `Typecore.type_effect_cases`** was merged via [#14723](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14723) by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin).
- **`translmod.ml` refactoring** to introduce a `lambda_subst` helper was merged via [#14728](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14728) by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb), suggested by [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch).
- **Regression test for rectypes keep-expansion** was merged via [#14730](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14730) by [Brandon Stride](https://github.com/brandonzstride), preventing re-introduction of a typechecker infinite loop.
- **Typechecker memory usage fix** for counter-example search was proposed via [#14727](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14727) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), addressing excessive memory use in pattern-matching exhaustiveness checks.
- A series of **ephemeron runtime refactoring PRs** ([#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732), [#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733), [#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734), [#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735)) were opened by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) to clarify phase-change logic and fix the ephemeron orphaning correctness bug on trunk.
repositories.discussions_summary: A deep technical discussion explored when and why orphaned ephemerons are adopted across GC cycle boundaries in the multicore runtime.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Ephemeron orphaning and adoption semantics** were dissected in [#14720](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/discussions/14720) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), questioning whether orphaned ephemerons should ever survive across GC cycles and proposing simplifications; the thread involves [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez), [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk), [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes), and [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan).
- **Adding extension points to the AST** for ppxlib compatibility was discussed in [#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668) by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb), exploring how to encode new language features without breaking existing ppx-es, with [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) committing to a concrete proposal.
- **Misleading syntactic arity error message** was reported in [#14719](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14719) by [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop), showing a spurious error for valid functions with module-dependent arrows, prompting a fix PR [#14721](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14721) from [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
- **`Atomic.fold_update`** naming debate continued on [#14725](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14725), with [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) and [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) preferring `modify_get` or `map_update` over `fold_update`.
- **Stdlib oversights around `iarray`** continued active in [#14320](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14320) by [Andreas Rossberg](https://github.com/rossberg), noting that `Iarray.sub` has a divergent signature and `Dynarray.to/of_iarray` are missing, with [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) pointing to [#14724](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14724) as a partial fix.
repositories.trends_summary: The week saw a focus on long-pending typechecker correctness fixes, ephemeron GC stability in multicore scenarios, and stdlib completeness for newer data structures.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Typechecker correctness and well-foundedness**: the landmark **keep expansion** merge [#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) signals a major push to fix deep-seated non-termination and principality bugs accumulated over years, with more follow-on work expected around class types and recursive modules.
- **Ephemeron GC hardening**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened a multi-PR series ([#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732)–[#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735)) to systematically clarify and fix ephemeron lifecycle management, motivated by [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid)'s multicoretests reproducer in [#14349](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14349).
- **Stdlib completeness for immutable and dynamic arrays**: the addition of `pp_print_iarray` [#14724](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14724) and `pp_print_dynarray` [#14726](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14726) reflects ongoing work to bring `iarray` and `Dynarray` to feature parity with `Array`.
- **Security hygiene in CI**: PR [#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14676) by [Austin Theriault](https://github.com/ajbt200128) proposed preventing shell injection in GitHub Actions workflows, reflecting broader supply-chain security concerns.
repositories.contributors_summary: Key contributors this week include Gabriel Scherer, Jacques Garrigue, Stefan Muenzel, Florian Angeletti, and Olivier Nicole.
repositories.contributors:
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) was the most active contributor, merging [#14716](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14716) (domain unique_id), [#14713](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14713) (ocamltest cold predicate), [#10798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10798) (Atomic.update), and [#14685](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14685) (compaction test fix), and opening the ephemeron refactoring series.
- [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) drove the landmark merge of [#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) (keep expansion), closing multiple long-standing typechecker correctness issues.
- [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) co-authored the keep-expansion work [#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) and merged the GADT scope-escape hint [#14695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14695).
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) merged primitive aliases [#13377](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13377) and opened the typechecker memory fix [#14727](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14727).
- [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) contributed `Format.pp_print_iarray` [#14724](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14724) and `Format.pp_print_dynarray` [#14726](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14726).
- [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) landed primitive aliases (RFC #44) via [#13377](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13377) after an extended review period.
- [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) contributed the minor cleanup [#14723](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14723) in effect-case typechecking.
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) contributed the `lambda_subst` refactoring [#14728](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14728).
- [Brandon Stride](https://github.com/brandonzstride) added the rectypes keep-expansion regression test [#14730](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14730).
- [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) provided invaluable multicoretests reproduction cases that drove the ephemeron debugging effort around [#14349](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14349).

repositories.brief_summary: Three PRs opened for GC corruption fix, OCaml 4.08-4.10 build support removal, and dependency cleanup; PGP key expiry resolved.
repositories.activity_summary: Three build/correctness PRs opened by Kate and Anil Madhavapeddy, with ongoing work on Nushell support and repository performance.
repositories.activity:
- **GC corruption fix** in the `uname` FFI binding by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened as [#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880), registering intermediate strings as OCaml GC roots to prevent potential memory corruption; the fix was reported by [Andrew Nesbitt](https://github.com/andrew)
- **Removal of OCaml 4.08, 4.09, and 4.10 build support** proposed by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) in [#6879](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6879), cleaning up 22 files across 150 lines of compatibility code, contingent on the opam-repository minimum compiler bump to 4.11
- **Dependency lower bounds spring cleanup** opened by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) as [#6878](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6878), adding missing lower bounds across build dependencies and including a helper script for installing autoconf 2.71
- **Nushell shell support** PR [#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6873) by [@jneem](https://github.com/jneem) remains open with discussion on minimum supported Nushell version; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) suggested targeting Nushell 0.104 (the version in Alpine 3.22) as a reasonable lower bound given `source null` requires 0.102+
- **HTTP repository performance overhaul** draft PR [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) received input from [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) on streaming tar.gz generation to reduce memory usage
- **Action reordering by priority** draft PR [#6864](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6864) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) was updated with a PoC extending the approach to differentiate installs, upgrades, and removals separately, fixing [#6863](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6863)
- **Expired PGP signing key** reported in [#6877](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6877) by [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) was resolved: [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) pointed to the in-progress key update at [ocaml-opam/opam2web#254](https://github.com/ocaml-opam/opam2web/issues/254) and [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) confirmed the updated key at `opam.ocaml.org/opam-dev-pubkey.pgp` is live
repositories.discussions_summary: PGP key expiry for opam release binaries was reported and resolved; DockerHub image maintenance scope clarified.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) reported in [#6877](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6877) that the opam release signing key had expired (originally valid until 2025-12-16); [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) referenced the key renewal work at [ocaml-opam/opam2web#254](https://github.com/ocaml-opam/opam2web/issues/254) and [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) confirmed the new key was live and closed the issue
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) raised [#6875](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6875) about DockerHub `ocaml/opam` images shipping outdated opam 2.0.10; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) clarified that the opam team does not maintain those images and directed future reports to [ocurrent/docker-base-images](https://github.com/ocurrent/docker-base-images/issues)
- In [#6879](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6879), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) noted that removing OCaml 4.08-4.10 build support is safe regardless of the opam-repository minimum compiler bump, since newer opam clients can still manage switches running those older compilers
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing effort to reduce technical debt in build tooling and improve correctness of C FFI bindings.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Build tooling debt reduction** is a recurring theme: [#6879](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6879) drops older compiler targets and [#6878](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6878) tightens dependency lower bounds, continuing a pattern of narrowing the supported build surface
- **GC safety in C stubs** continues to surface as an area of concern; the uname fix in [#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880) mirrors earlier GC root registration fixes, suggesting a broader audit of FFI code may be worthwhile
- **Shell environment integration** is expanding beyond POSIX shells, with [#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6873) adding Nushell support following the earlier fish/zsh improvements
repositories.contributors_summary: Kate and Anil Madhavapeddy opened new PRs; Kate reviewed and discussed ongoing work across multiple issues and PRs.
repositories.contributors:
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened three PRs ([#6879](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6879), [#6878](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6878)) for build improvements, updated the draft action-reordering PR [#6864](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6864) with a revised PoC, responded to the PGP key issue [#6877](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6877) confirming the fix, clarified Docker image ownership in [#6875](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6875), and provided Nushell version guidance in [#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6873)
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened [#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880) fixing GC root registration in the uname C stub and commented in [#6879](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6879) on backward compatibility implications
- [@jneem](https://github.com/jneem) continued work on Nushell shell integration in [#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6873)
- [@dinosaure](https://github.com/dinosaure) contributed design feedback on tar.gz streaming in the repository performance PR [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)
- [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu) reported the expired PGP signing key in [#6877](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6877)
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) triaged [#6877](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6877) by linking the key renewal PR
- [Reynir Björnsson](https://github.com/reynir) commented in [#6600](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6600) noting the missing source directory issue appeared to self-resolve after `opam update`

repositories.brief_summary: ocaml-lsp 1.26.0 released with new custom requests, rangeFormatting, and alternative build system support.
repositories.new_features_summary: Release 1.26.0 ships refactorExtract, destruct, locate_types requests, rangeFormatting, and custom build system config.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`ocamllsp/refactorExtract`** custom request enables extract-to-variable/function refactoring workflows in editors ([#1545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1545))
- New **`destruct`** custom request exposes Merlin's pattern-match destruction capability via LSP ([#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583))
- New **`locate_types`** custom request allows editors to locate type definitions ([#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584))
- Added support for the standard **`textDocument/rangeFormatting`** LSP request, enabling format-on-selection in editors ([#1591](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1591))
- **Alternative build system support** via `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM` and `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_ROOT` environment variables — when set, ocaml-lsp will invoke the specified build system command to obtain Merlin configuration using `merlin-dot-protocol`, allowing use outside of Dune projects ([#1600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1600))
- Fixed **hover** availability on class and object related locations that were previously unsupported ([#1599](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1599))
- Changelog and version metadata updated for the **1.26.0 release** by [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) and released by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) ([#1600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1600))
repositories.activity_summary: Two PRs remain open: a Merlin short-paths config fix and an automated Nix flake lock update.
repositories.activity:
- Open PR by [Gaëtan Gilbert](https://github.com/SkySkimmer) fixing **incorrect `-short-paths` config propagation** for Merlin, where the default was not set to `real_paths = true` from `Mconfig.initial` before `get_external_config` processes OCaml flags — labeled `consider-for-LTS` and awaiting review by @voodoos ([#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1579))
- Automated **Nix flake lock update** PR bumping `merlin5_3` (to commit from 2026-04-09) and `nixpkgs`/`nix-overlays` remains open, pending maintainer merge ([#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437))
- [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) merged the **changelog update for 1.26.0** with [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) noting that merlin-lib constraints also need updating before the release ([#1600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1600))
repositories.discussions_summary: voodoos noted merlin-lib constraints need updating alongside the 1.26.0 changelog PR.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) commented on [#1600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1600) that **merlin-lib version constraints** need updating in the opam file before the 1.26.0 release can be finalized, indicating a follow-up task tied to the release process
- [Gaëtan Gilbert](https://github.com/SkySkimmer) pinged [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) on the open **short-paths fix** [#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1579), which has been open since December 2025 and targets a correctness issue in Merlin configuration handling
repositories.trends_summary: The 1.26.0 release reflects a pattern of expanding custom LSP request coverage and improving editor tool integration.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Expanding **custom LSP request surface** continues: `refactorExtract`, `destruct`, and `locate_types` join an existing set of ocaml-lsp-specific extensions, suggesting a deliberate effort to expose Merlin capabilities via the LSP protocol ([#1545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1545), [#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583), [#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584))
- **Build system agnosticism** is a new direction: the `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM` support breaks the previous assumption that only Dune projects are supported, opening the server to other OCaml build systems that implement `merlin-dot-protocol` ([#1600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1600))
repositories.contributors_summary: PixieDust prepared the 1.26.0 release changelog; voodoos published the release and noted follow-up work.
repositories.contributors:
- [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) authored and merged the changelog update for the 1.26.0 release ([#1600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1600))
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) published the [1.26.0 release](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/releases/tag/1.26.0) and provided a review comment on [#1600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1600) flagging the need to update merlin-lib constraints
- [Gaëtan Gilbert](https://github.com/SkySkimmer) has an open PR [#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1579) fixing short-paths configuration for Merlin and pinged maintainers for review this week

repositories.brief_summary: Release v5.7.0-504 shipped with new refactor-extract-region command and signature help improvements.
repositories.new_features_summary: v5.7.0-504 release includes new refactor-extract-region command, locate-types and signature help improvements.
repositories.new_features:
- New experimental **`refactor-extract-region` command** for extracting a region of code into a fresh `let` binding, released in [v5.7.0-504](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/releases/tag/v5.7.0-504) ([#1948](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1948))
- **`locate-types` result** gains an `"Other"` variant to handle types that don't fit existing categories ([#2025](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2025)), and now omits `option` from results for optional parameters ([#2027](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2027))
- New **`-end-position` parameter** for the `enclosing` command, allowing callers to specify an end position ([#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029))
- **Signature help** improvements: no longer loops over parameters after completion ([#2023](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2023)), fixes for labelled and optional parameter handling ([#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032)), and now triggers correctly when the `in` keyword has not been written yet ([#2036](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2036))
- Improved **type enclosing** behavior across class and object-related items ([#2053](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2053))
- Fixed **record field autocompletion** regression ([#2028](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2028))
- **`PATH_MAX`** is now defined to 4096 when undefined (e.g. on GNU Hurd), fixing a portability issue in the merlin binary ([#2039](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2039))
- Changelog formatting fixes committed directly: added missing markdown heading underlines and fixed `jq` command quoting ([6361a4e](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/commit/6361a4e8027d0207d2a947d13611402b36ba3460), [0dae64e](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/commit/0dae64e0c54a7ccddaa042d98edd1aa834fb31ca))
repositories.activity_summary: Two PRs remain open: LRU cache for indexer memory and support for pointing ocaml-index files.
repositories.activity:
- **[#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051) — support for pointing indexes in ocaml-index** remains open, opened by [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d); active review discussion with [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) and [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) focusing on store ID validation, error reporting for outdated builds, and handling of relative vs absolute file paths
- **[#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050) — LRU cache for indexer** remains open, opened by [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo); replaces an unbounded `Hashtbl` with an LRU cache to bound memory growth in the ocaml-index component
- **[#2055](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2055) — changelog update** for v5.7.0-504 was opened by [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) and merged by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos), who then cut the [v5.7.0-504 release](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/releases/tag/v5.7.0-504)
repositories.discussions_summary: Review discussion on PR #2051 covers store ID validation design, outdated-build error reporting, and path resolution.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Store ID validation design** in [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051): [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) raised concerns about placing the `id` field directly in the `store` type rather than in `On_disk`, to better reflect its role in identifying the expected index file; the discussion resolved toward moving the check into `force_open_store`
- **Outdated build error reporting** in [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051): [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) asked for an explicit exception type (e.g. `Outdated_store`) with a user-visible message when the store file is stale or missing, so that tools can signal rebuild requirements; [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) suggested the variant `Outdated_store of [ \`Missing_file of string | \`Index_ids_do_not_match ]`
- **Relative vs absolute path handling** in [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051): [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) questioned whether pointed filenames could break when `ocaml-index` is called from different directories; [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) clarified that filenames are relative to the build context root (e.g. `_build/default`) when used with Dune
repositories.trends_summary: Active development on ocaml-index infrastructure: memory efficiency and on-disk index referencing are both in progress.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **ocaml-index infrastructure improvements** are a recurring theme, with two concurrent open PRs ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050), [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)) targeting memory efficiency (LRU caching) and storage architecture (pointing to existing on-disk index files), suggesting continued investment in scalable indexing
- **Signature help robustness** has seen multiple fixes in the v5.7.0-504 release ([#2023](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2023), [#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032), [#2036](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2036)), indicating systematic work on edge cases in this feature area
repositories.contributors_summary: PixieDust prepared the release, Ulysse cut it, art-w reviewed PR #2051, Tim ats and Charlène_Gros drove open PRs.
repositories.contributors:
- [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) opened and had merged the changelog update PR [#2055](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2055) in preparation for the v5.7.0-504 release
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) merged [#2055](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2055), cut the [v5.7.0-504 release](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/releases/tag/v5.7.0-504), and participated in code review on [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051) with guidance on exception type design and path handling
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) provided detailed review on [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051), covering store ID validation, error reporting design, `int` vs `int64` encoding, and relative path concerns
- [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) is the author of [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051) and addressed multiple rounds of review feedback during the week
- [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) opened [#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050) introducing LRU-based memory management for the indexer

repositories.brief_summary: Dune 3.22.2 was released, the RPC and scheduler internals saw extensive consolidation, and Rocq/Melange support received multiple fixes.
repositories.new_features_summary: New commands and build tracing capabilities were added, including watch-mode trace events, a sexp pretty-printer, and a start-file initializer.
repositories.new_features:
- [#14163](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14163) adds trace events for watch-mode iterations (build start, restart, finish), enabling better profiling of incremental builds by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [#14083](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14083) introduces `dune init start-file` to scaffold the recommended entry point for driving dune in watch mode by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [#14068](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14068) adds `dune internal sexp-pp`, an internal command to pretty-print s-expressions useful for debugging dune files by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [#14071](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14071) adds a `--deps` flag to `dune rules` to print the dependencies of a rule directly by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [#14073](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14073) adds the ability to dump the digest database for debugging stale-dependency investigations by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [#13997](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13997) adds an `extracted_files` field to the `rocq.extraction` stanza, preventing extracted non-OCaml files (e.g. `.hs`) from being wiped on subsequent builds by [Will Thomas](https://github.com/Durbatuluk1701)
- [#13794](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13794) allows writing `ppx_expect` tests in the bootstrap layer by stripping `let%expect_test` nodes at compile time, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [#14059](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14059) extends the engine's copy action to support directory targets, a prerequisite for cleaner Melange runtime asset promotion by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
repositories.activity_summary: Active work continues on per-module inter-library dependency filtering and on restructuring the RPC and scheduler packages into a flatter module hierarchy.
repositories.activity:
- [#14116](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14116) proposes per-module inter-library dependency filtering using ocamldep output to eliminate unnecessary recompilation when an unused module in a dependency library changes; under review by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb)
- [#14144](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14144) proposes an `(archived false)` field on the `(library)` stanza to allow linking library objects directly without generating `.cma`/`.cmxa` archives, by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb)
- [#13792](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13792) resolves directory symlinks in fetched package sources to avoid breakage in the build sandbox, by [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS)
- [#14118](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14118) removes support for old-style implicit directory targets; pending a revdep check by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [#14108](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14108) switches opam file generation from `(mode promote)` to a `diff` action so that `@opam`, `@runtest`, and `@install` all trigger the check, merged shortly after the week by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- Multiple RPC library consolidation PRs landed: [#14147](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14147) merges `csexp_rpc` into `rpc`, [#14145](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14145) merges `dune_rpc_client` and `dune_rpc_server`, [#14153](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14153) merges `fsevents` into `dune_scheduler`, and [#14155](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14155) merges `fswatch_win` into `dune_scheduler`, all by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [#14093](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14093) fixes Rocq support for version 9.0+ by switching from `rocq --config` to `rocq c --config`, by [Will Thomas](https://github.com/Durbatuluk1701)
- [#14066](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14066) fixes autolocking so that changes to `(depends)` in `dune-project` are detected in watch mode, closing [#13234](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13234) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [#14078](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14078) adds opam packaging checks to CI so that `opam lint` failures on `.opam` files are caught early on every PR that touches them, by [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247)
repositories.discussions_summary: The Dune monitor's long-term architecture and the policy on what may be included in patch releases were discussed this week.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#14121](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14121) outlines the long-term roadmap for `dune monitor`: moving it to a separate binary, replacing Fiber with Lwt, unvendoring Notty/Lwd, and having it communicate over RPC and trace files by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [#14112](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14112) documents the 3.22.2 release process; [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) explained the policy of restricting patch releases to regressions only, to maintain release cadence and avoid introducing new regressions through backports
- [#13682](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13682) on adding `(excluded_modules)` to the library stanza was closed after [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) and [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) discussed a broader `(archived false)` direction and the alternative of first-class module group stanzas, leading to [#14144](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14144)
- [#14095](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14095) proposes deprecating `(wrapped (compat ...))` now that `(wrapped false)` combined with `(include_subdirs qualified)` covers the same use case
repositories.trends_summary: The week shows a sustained effort to flatten the internal package structure and reduce indirection, particularly in the scheduler and RPC subsystems.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) is systematically collapsing previously separate libraries (`fsevents`, `fswatch_win`, `csexp_rpc`, `dune_rpc_client`, `dune_rpc_server`) into their natural homes (`dune_scheduler`, `rpc`), removing abstraction layers that added complexity without benefit
- The release cadence initiative ([#13771](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13771)) is gaining tooling support: [#14120](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14120) adds leaf packages with many reverse-dependencies to the `rc` revdep check, [#14062](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14062) runs revdeps on `rc` branches with dev tools, and [#14115](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14115) is a WIP script to automate the release kick-off
- Build tracing is expanding: lock-dir load events ([#14064](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14064)), watch-mode iteration events ([#14163](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14163)), and digest database dumps ([#14073](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14073)) are all landing in the same week
- Rocq support is being actively maintained by [Will Thomas](https://github.com/Durbatuluk1701), with both a compatibility fix ([#14093](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14093)) and a doc overhaul ([#14167](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14167)) this week
repositories.issues_summary: Several long-standing issues were closed after community contributors verified them as no longer reproducible or already fixed.
repositories.good_first_issues:
- [#14096](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14096) reports that `%{pkg:foo:lib}` inside a `(run)` action in a lock file incorrectly expands to a sandbox path rather than a relative install path, reported by [@Alizter](https://github.com/Alizter)
- [#14061](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14061) proposes a new `(imported_modules)` field on the `(library)` stanza to teach dune about the import-module alias pattern so that per-module dependency filtering can handle it correctly, filed by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [#14044](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14044) proposes deprecating `(mode promote)` in favour of `(diff)` and per-request build promotion, with [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) noting that diff rules already cover the main use case
repositories.contributors_summary: Contributions this week came from both core team members and external contributors, with Rudi Grinberg and Robin Bate Boerop being especially active.
repositories.contributors:
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) drove the bulk of the scheduler/RPC consolidation and multiple new features
- [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) landed a series of test and fix PRs ([#14146](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14146), [#14137](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14137), [#14136](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14136), [#14135](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14135)) and continues developing the per-module dependency filtering work in [#14116](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14116)
- [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) fixed transitive `.cmj` handling ([#14119](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14119)) and landed directory-target support for Melange ([#14059](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14059), [#14148](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14148), [#14150](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14150))
- [Will Thomas](https://github.com/Durbatuluk1701) contributed Rocq compatibility and documentation improvements ([#14093](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14093), [#13997](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13997), [#14167](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14167))
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) managed the 3.22.2 release process and continues work on release automation ([#14115](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14115))
- [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) added high-revdep leaf packages to the CI revdep check ([#14120](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14120)) and backported the `--diff-command` fix ([#14124](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14124))
- [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) landed opam packaging checks in CI for PRs ([#14078](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14078))
- [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) continued work on symlink resolution in fetched sources ([#13792](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13792)) and removed a dead `follow_symlinks` function ([#14091](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14091))
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) opened the `(archived false)` proposal ([#14144](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14144))

repositories.brief_summary: Design discussion on implicit source positions RFC (#52) continued with focus on implementation alternatives.
repositories.activity_summary: Ongoing design discussion on the implicit source positions RFC with commentary on implementation variants.
repositories.activity: - The **implicit source positions** RFC ([#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)) opened by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) continued to receive design feedback, with [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) expressing a preference for design alternatives 2a or 2b, noting that 2a would likely be acceptable performance-wise and could serve as a stepping stone toward 2b; [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) agreed with this direction.
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on RFC #52 narrowed toward design alternatives 2a/2b for implicit call-site position tracking.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) discussed **implementation strategy** for the `[%call_pos]` feature in [#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52), with a preference for option 2a as an incremental first step before pursuing the more complete 2b approach.
repositories.contributors_summary: Gabriel Scherer reviewed and commented on the implicit source positions RFC; Olivier Nicole responded to design feedback.
repositories.contributors:
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) reviewed the **implicit source positions** RFC ([#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)) and provided design guidance on preferred implementation alternatives.
- [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) authored [#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) and responded to review comments, acknowledging the suggested incremental approach.

repositories.brief_summary: A proof-of-concept @inline tag PR and a Markdown frontmatter feature request highlighted the week.
repositories.activity_summary: New PR for @inline module support and a Markdown frontmatter issue opened, with ongoing review of OCaml 5.5 and OxCaml PRs.
repositories.activity:
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) opened [#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408) as a **proof-of-concept `@inline` tag** for module declarations, addressing the long-standing request in [#564](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/564); when a module's doc comment includes `@inline`, its contents are rendered directly on the parent page rather than a separate sub-page, reusing the existing rendering path for inlined includes — the PR was generated using Claude Code to gauge implementation effort and was closed without merging after review
- [David Sancho](https://github.com/davesnx) opened [#1409](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1409) proposing that the **Markdown backend support line tags as frontmatter fields**, noting that `@short_title` and `@children_order` would map naturally to frontmatter keys and querying whether this should be opt-in or default
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) marked [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) (**OCaml 5.5 compatibility**) as ready for review; the PR adds support for external types (rendered as abstract) and module arrows introduced in OCaml 5.5
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) addressed review feedback on [#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399) (**Upstream OxCaml**), removing an unnecessary type annotation flagged by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam)
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on issue #564 revisited the trade-offs of allowing per-module inline rendering vs. a uniform URL structure.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [#564](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/564) (**Support inlining expansions in their parent's page**) saw renewed discussion sparked by the opening of [#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408); commenters debated whether per-author control over inlining leads to inconsistent documentation experiences across the ecosystem, with [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) arguing that user-controlled inlining breaks predictable URL structures and [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) suggesting sane defaults with opt-in overrides
repositories.trends_summary: Efforts to broaden odoc's OCaml version coverage (5.5, OxCaml) and extend the Markdown backend are converging.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Expanding OCaml version compatibility** is a recurring theme, with both [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) (OCaml 5.5 new type constructs) and [#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399) (OxCaml extensions) progressing in parallel, suggesting the team is investing in keeping odoc current with upstream and downstream compiler variants
- **Markdown backend feature parity** with the HTML backend is being explored, as [#1409](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1409) proposes bringing line-tag semantics into the Markdown frontend via frontmatter, signalling growing interest in odoc as a documentation source for non-HTML consumers
repositories.contributors_summary: Martin Jambon opened the @inline proof-of-concept PR; David Sancho filed a Markdown frontmatter feature request.
repositories.contributors:
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) opened [#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408) with a full proof-of-concept implementation of `@inline` module support (757 additions across 30 files) and participated in the discussion on [#564](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/564)
- [David Sancho](https://github.com/davesnx) filed [#1409](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1409) requesting Markdown frontmatter support for odoc line tags
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) marked [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) ready for review and engaged in discussion about removing the odoc-parser upper bound with [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam)
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) reviewed both [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) and [#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399), approving [#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399) after [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) addressed his comments
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) responded to review feedback on [#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399) and pushed fixes
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) reviewed [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) and suggested adding a test case for polymorphic parameters

repositories.brief_summary: Content week with new Planet posts, job listings, release changelogs, and two tutorial PRs opened for review.
repositories.new_features_summary: New OCaml Planet posts, Cloud Software Group job listing, and release changelogs for dune 3.22.1, ocaml-lsp 1.26.0, and merlin 5.7.0 added.
repositories.new_features:
- New **OCaml Planet blog posts** scraped across two automated updates, including posts from [Thomas Gazagnaire](https://github.com/samoht) on predicting satellite collisions in OCaml, anil on AI screen time and the need for an antibotty immune system, tunbury on OCI image exports, a CVE fix that broke CI, coding a digital clock in OCaml 5 on Raspberry Pi Pico 2W, and the OCaml Weekly News digest ([#3597](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3597), [#3602](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3602), [#3604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3604))
- New **job listing** added for a Lead Systems Software Engineer position at Cloud Software Group (Remote / United Kingdom) by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) ([#3600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3600))
- **Release changelogs** added for ocaml-lsp 1.26.0 and merlin 5.7.0-504 by [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) ([#3605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3605)), and a dune 3.22.1 release announcement by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([#3596](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3596))
- **Security page** updated to reflect [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok)'s affiliation change to Tarides ([#3606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3606))
- **Job offer at Inria Rennes** on static analysis of OCaml programs (Salto project) added by [Benoit Montagu](https://github.com/esope) ([#3601](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3601))
repositories.activity_summary: Two substantial tutorial PRs opened for continuous benchmarking and OCaml-CI self-hosting, alongside a note on combobulate Emacs integration.
repositories.activity:
- **Continuous benchmarking tutorial** opened by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) covering the current-bench tool, JSON output format, the cobench library, hosted setup at bench.ci.dev, self-hosting with Docker Compose, API submission, and the dashboard/PR workflow ([#3598](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3598))
- **Self-hosting section** for the OCaml-CI tutorial opened by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) covering building Docker images, creating a GitHub App with required permissions, Docker Swarm secrets, and stack deployment ([#3599](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3599))

- **Automated Planet scraping** continued across three separate PRs adding diverse community content to the OCaml Planet feed ([#3597](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3597), [#3602](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3602), [#3604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3604))
repositories.trends_summary: Tutorial expansion is ongoing, with two new platform workflow tutorials (continuous benchmarking, CI self-hosting) opened for review this week.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Platform tutorial expansion** continues with [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) opening two new tutorials on continuous benchmarking with current-bench and self-hosting OCaml-CI ([#3598](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3598), [#3599](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3599)), extending the existing platform toolchain documentation
- **Editor tooling documentation** is expanding with [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) adding coverage for combobulate Emacs integration ([#3603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3603)), part of a broader effort to keep editor setup guides current with ecosystem developments
repositories.contributors_summary: Six contributors were active this week through content additions, release announcements, job postings, and tutorial work.
repositories.contributors:
- [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) added a Cloud Software Group job listing ([#3600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3600)) and opened two tutorial PRs for continuous benchmarking and OCaml-CI self-hosting ([#3598](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3598), [#3599](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3599))

- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) contributed the dune 3.22.1 release announcement ([#3596](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3596))
- [Benoit Montagu](https://github.com/esope) added an Inria Rennes job offer for static analysis of OCaml programs ([#3601](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3601))
- [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) updated the security page to reflect his move to Tarides ([#3606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3606))
- Automated **GitHub Actions** scraping bot added new OCaml Planet community posts across three PRs ([#3597](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3597), [#3602](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3602), [#3604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3604))

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with major releases including dune 3.22.2, atd 4.1.0, merlin 5.7.0-504, and ocaml-lsp 1.26.0, plus many new package submissions.
repositories.new_features_summary: New packages span AI SDK bindings, cryptography, multimedia streaming, YAML parsing, and spacecraft trajectory visualization.
repositories.new_features:
- New **ocaml-ai-sdk** (0.1) by [José Nogueira](https://github.com/thatportugueseguy): type-safe provider-agnostic AI SDK with Anthropic and OpenAI backends ([#29679](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29679))

- New **atd-jsonlike** and **atd-yamlx** packages as part of atd 4.1.0 by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon): generic JSON-like AST with source locations and YAML-to-ATD bridge ([#29709](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29709))
- New **ezlua** (0.1) by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube): OCaml-Lua codec runtime and high-level interpreter API ([#29655](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29655))
- New **ephemeral** (1.0) by [Charles Averill](https://github.com/CharlesAverill): spacecraft trajectory visualization library, initial release ([#29693](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29693))
- New **jsonschema-core** and **jsonschema-validation** (0.1.0) by [Yuriy Krasilnikov](https://github.com/YuriyKrasilnikov): JSON Schema draft-07 validation with $ref resolution ([#29664](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29664))
- New **idna** (0.3.0) by [Yuriy Krasilnikov](https://github.com/YuriyKrasilnikov): pure-OCaml IDNA2008 hostname validation with Unicode 16.0.0 tables ([#29680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29680))
- New **layoutz** (0.1.0) by [Matthieu Court](https://github.com/mattlianje): Elm-style TUI runtime for terminal user interfaces ([#29665](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29665))
- New **snkv** (0.1.1) by [Tomasz Barański](https://github.com/tomob): OCaml FFI bindings for SNKV, a persistent ACID-compliant key-value store on SQLite's B-tree engine ([#29681](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29681))
- New **sm** (0.1.1) by [sang](https://github.com/daysv): Pure OCaml Chinese National Cryptographic Algorithm Library (SM2/SM3/SM4/ZUC) with performance optimizations ([#29698](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29698))
- New **tsdl-ttf** (0.7) by [珊瑚](https://github.com/sanette): SDL2_ttf bindings to use with Tsdl ([#29648](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29648))
- New **bin_there** and **ppx_bin_there** (0.1.12) by [David Teller](https://github.com/Yoric): serialization/deserialization with support for shared values and PPX deriving ([#29677](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29677))

repositories.discussions_summary: OCaml 4.14.3 compatibility issues dominated discussions, with js_of_ocaml-compiler and stdcompat receiving incompatibility constraints.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- OCaml 4.14.3 compatibility issues: multiple packages (js_of_ocaml-compiler, stdcompat) received incompatibility constraints due to the removal of `ce_rec` field in `Instruct.compilation_env` ([#29692](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29692), [#29667](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29667))
- **dune 3.22.1** reported as failing to build with OCaml 4.14.0 by [Ralf Jung](https://github.com/RalfJung); investigation found it was user-environment specific and not a general OCaml 4 regression ([#29658](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29658))
- **lwt6** constraints refinement PR opened by [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) with 84 changed files, awaiting CI results ([#29530](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29530))
- **MSVC Windows** build issues: mingw dependencies incorrectly pulled in for MSVC builds of conf-cairo, conf-gtk3, conf-pkg-config; reported by [Tõivo Leedjärv](https://github.com/tleedjarv) ([#29609](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29609))
- **conf-libffi** Windows fix by [Puneeth Chaganti](https://github.com/punchagan): added mingw-w64-shims as direct build dependency to prevent ordering issues on Windows ([#29691](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29691))
- **camlidl** Windows patch by [Boris Yakobowski](https://github.com/yakobowski): fixed Windows Makefile extension and GCC 14 build failures, establishing policy of using patch versions (e.g. 1.13-1) for patched packages ([#29653](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29653))
- **piqi** build failures investigated by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV): added availability constraints to fix C binding issues with newer compilers ([#29701](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29701))
repositories.trends_summary: Several new packages address AI/LLM integration, cryptography standards, and multimedia processing, reflecting growing OCaml ecosystem breadth.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- AI SDK tooling arriving in the OCaml ecosystem: **ocaml-ai-sdk** provides a Vercel AI SDK-inspired abstraction layer for Anthropic Claude and OpenAI GPT models
- Chinese national cryptographic standards (SM2/SM3/SM4/ZUC) now available as a pure OCaml library (**sm**), expanding cryptographic coverage
- Pure-OCaml YAML processing gaining a new comprehensive option (**yamlx**) that passes all official YAML conformance tests without C bindings
- Ongoing OCaml 4.14.3 compatibility work across the ecosystem, with multiple packages receiving constraint updates due to internal OCaml API changes
- Windows MSVC build support improvements, with several conf packages being fixed to avoid incorrect mingw dependency pulls
- Multimedia streaming ecosystem active: both **liquidsoap** and **ocaml-ffmpeg** released new versions in the same week

repositories.brief_summary: Wasm dynlink and toplevel support landed, with two follow-up Wasm size-reduction PRs opened.
repositories.new_features_summary: Wasm backend gains dynlink, toplevel, virtual filesystem, Graphics primitives, and new CLI flags.
repositories.new_features:
- **Wasm dynlink support** merged via [#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187): `wasm_of_ocaml` can now dynamically load pre-compiled `.wasmo`/`.wasma` files, perform in-memory compilation from bytecode to Wasm, and support OCaml's standard `Dynlink.loadfile` API
- New **`--dynlink` CLI flag** added to `wasm_of_ocaml` to enable dynamic linking mode ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187), commit [9d1a3a3](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/9d1a3a398895f0d9e7d99e045be54b0023148339))
- New **`--toplevel`, `--no-cmis`, and `--export` CLI flags** added to `wasm_of_ocaml` for building interactive toplevel applications with configurable CMI embedding and export control ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187), commit [ef00f8a](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/ef00f8ae3ee1f2fdd4edce5e41f9f5ac67c61cfe))
- New **`Wasm_of_ocaml_compiler_dynlink` library** introduced with a `loadfile : string -> unit` API for loading pre-compiled Wasm modules at runtime ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187), commit [b3f2ab3](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/b3f2ab318caf7c5c5803711b3968e946386cb728))
- **Virtual filesystem** support added to the Wasm runtime, enabling file-system-dependent OCaml code to run in Wasm environments ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187), commit [c1767d6](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/c1767d672402e03703a6c2ad918a5e9027f9d80c))
- **Graphics library primitives** added to the Wasm runtime, allowing graphics-using OCaml programs to run under `wasm_of_ocaml` ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187), commit [709ee8f](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/709ee8fb3e9ffbfdd49ab6aa822ffa37aeaae724))
- **`caml_ml_set_channel_output` and `caml_ml_set_channel_refill`** implemented in the Wasm runtime for custom channel I/O redirection ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187), commit [5f01fd5](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/5f01fd57cbc804781d086c717c20f0bdd4fff0aa))
- **`caml_ba_kind_of_typed_array`, `caml_list_mount_point`, and `caml_unmount`** implemented in the Wasm runtime ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187), commit [07d8515](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/07d85158dcea3f49b3ab9c07bfb2fadc96ab36e8))
- **Toplevel sublibrary** refactored into a backend-neutral component, enabling reuse across both JS and Wasm backends ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187), commit [5b5cb70](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/5b5cb709f8051b3d2d1e7bb5fdbd9348114a2a1f))
- **All bundled examples** now work with the Wasm backend, including a Lwt toplevel example with Wasm support ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187), commits [bc061ed](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/bc061ed41d04b61c11f5e41526df95e91e74148c), [cd146d8](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/cd146d887652316cfde739466bec6d108075abea))
- **Documentation updated** for Wasm dynlink, toplevel, and pseudo filesystem features ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187), commit [eae8d3b](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/eae8d3b0996d5edaac80c3932fd491d27a7617d2))
repositories.activity_summary: Two new Wasm size-reduction PRs opened following the dynlink/toplevel merge, both targeting toplevel function overhead.
repositories.activity:
- **Two new Wasm optimization PRs opened** by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) as follow-up work after the dynlink/toplevel merge: [#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211) puts constant expressions into global variables to reduce toplevel function size (halving it for the PRT benchmark), and [#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212) (draft) extracts loops from the toplevel function into helper functions to avoid wasteful Wasm engine tier-up of the large init function
- **Dynlink / toplevel support** ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)) merged after development since February, bringing 6383 additions across 139 files including predefined exception disambiguation in `caml_register_global`, `Global_name` module extraction, and `link_info` refactoring
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on PR #2212 raised whether loop extraction could benefit the JS backend as well.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) raised in [#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212) whether extracting loops from toplevel functions could benefit the JS backend in addition to Wasm, by allowing Wasm/JS engines to independently optimize extracted loop helpers
repositories.trends_summary: Sustained focus on Wasm backend parity and performance, with dynlink/toplevel completing a major capability gap.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Wasm backend capability parity** continues to be the primary development focus: the merged [#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187) closes a long-standing gap in dynlink and toplevel support, while the newly opened [#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211) and [#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212) immediately address size and performance costs introduced by the toplevel feature
- **Toplevel function size** is emerging as a specific optimization concern for the Wasm backend, with two independent approaches (global variable promotion and loop extraction) being explored in parallel
repositories.contributors_summary: Jérôme Vouillon was the sole contributor, merging dynlink/toplevel support and opening two follow-up optimization PRs.
repositories.contributors: - [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) merged the long-in-development Wasm dynlink and toplevel PR ([#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)) and opened two follow-up Wasm optimization PRs ([#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211), [#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212)); [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) reviewed [#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187) (approving the PR) and commented on [#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212) to raise the question of JS backend applicability

repositories.brief_summary: Active week across layout polymorphism, unboxed types, Flambda2 reaper, backend codegen, and runtime improvements.
repositories.new_features_summary: New language features landed including custom or_null types, layout-poly instantiation AST nodes, and a reversible name mangling scheme.
repositories.new_features:
- Custom user-defined `[@@or_null]` declarations with custom constructor names now supported, generalising the built-in `Null`/`This` representation [#5668](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5668)
- `Texp_apply_layout` node added to the typed AST for layout-polymorphic function instantiation [#5634](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5634)
- New reversible name mangling scheme added, enabling full module path reconstruction from mangled symbols [#5099](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5099)
- Small integer shift intrinsics added to the backend [#5814](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5814)
- `-Ix` flag added for include paths where `.cmx` files are guaranteed available, threading `cmx_guaranteed` info through the persistent env for future layout-poly use [#5654](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5654)
- n-way join now creates extra params for variables produced during the join, improving CSE precision [#5751](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5751)
- Bigarray construction routines now return `@ unique` values [#5767](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5767)
- Tick thread moved into the runtime [#5349](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5349)
- Prologue shrink-wrap enabled by default [#5762](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5762)
- `SPLIT_AROUND_LOOPS` regalloc parameter default flipped [#5761](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5761)
- Runtime 5 is now the default [#5780](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5780)
- Upgraded to ocamlformat 0.29.0, aligning with Merlin [#5777](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5777)
repositories.activity_summary: Layout polymorphism, Flambda2 reaper refactoring, unboxed type representation changes, and backend improvements were the main threads of activity.
repositories.activity:
- Draft SSA variant of the CFG backend opened by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) [#5810](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5810)
- Draft deletion of empty loops using induction variable analysis built on top of the SSA CFG, opened by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) [#5840](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5840)
- Skip `caml_modify` for `non_pointer` values opened by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), rebasing [Zack Eisbach](https://github.com/zackbach)'s earlier work [#5831](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5831)
- Represent all-value/void mixed records and variants as uniform blocks opened by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) [#5774](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5774)
- Represent `float64` records as mixed blocks and add `[@@represent_as_float_array]` attribute opened by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) [#5796](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5796)
- Require `[@@flatten_floats]` attribute on mixed `float`-`float64` records opened by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) [#5795](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5795)
- Move Flambda2 export info out of `.cmx` inline `Marshal` block into a section, opened by [Nicolas Trangez](https://github.com/NicolasT) [#5809](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5809)
- Structured Fexpr primitive representation for improved parameterization of fexprs opened by [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev) [#5825](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5825)
- Reaper code size computation when rebuilding terms opened by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) [#5793](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5793)
- Allow immediates in upstream-compatible code merged [#5699](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5699)
- `ikinds` on minus24 backend feature merged [#5159](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5159)
- Reaper: `dep_solver.ml` split into separate modules merged by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) [#5776](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5776)
- Reaper cleanup after dep_solver split merged [#5787](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5787)
- Patricia trees: additional `union_total` function families to avoid allocations merged [#5803](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5803)
- Simplify `lpoly` generalize via path compression merged by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) [#5792](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5792)
- Scannable axes compute nullability and separability merged [#5169](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5169)
- `update_level` corrected to ignore level when sort variable contents is `Some _` merged [#5791](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5791)
- Layout var instantiation changed to `fatal_error` rather than `Not_found` merged [#5817](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5817)
- `moregeneral` always restores `current_level` merged [#5816](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5816)
- Layout instantiation restricted to static values merged [#5783](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5783)
- Attempt to reset tick thread after `fork()` merged by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) [#5829](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5829)
- Additional compactions in the dissector merged [#5790](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5790)
- Fine-grained profiling added to the linker scan-file loop merged by [Nicolas Trangez](https://github.com/NicolasT) [#5808](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5808)
- Fix annoying REPL issue with metaprogramming syntax directive merged by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian) [#5801](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5801)
- Graceful reporting of unsupported language features in runtime metaprogramming merged [#5823](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5823)
- Handle staging in `Printtyp` more carefully merged by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) [#5785](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5785)
- Eliminate useless shifts in string/bytes indexed access merged [#5746](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5746)
- Share code in emitters for data items merged [#5794](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5794)
- Patricia tree callback modules for function specialisation merged [#5770](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5770)
- Remove line numbers from assertion failures in tests merged [#5782](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5782)
- Delete `equal_obj` and `equal_morph`, using compare instead merged [#5799](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5799)
- Review of Traverse and Traverse_acc modules merged [#5789](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5789) [#5797](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5797)
- 5.4 merge: various testsuite promotions merged [#5757](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5757)
- Fix statmemprof exception tests merged [#5804](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5804)
- Reaper: remove `field_{usages,sources}_top` merged [#5748](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5748)
- Simplify: update recursive flag to fix reaper inlining decision merged [#5819](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5819)
- Avoid persistent env wrapper partial applications, saving ~100mb allocation when compiling `typecore.ml` merged [#5631](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5631)
repositories.discussions_summary: Review activity focused on unboxed type representation changes, layout polymorphism correctness, and the Flambda2 export info refactoring.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#5831](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5831) (skip `caml_modify` for `non_pointer`) includes a detailed discussion of interactions with abstract kinds, type substitution edge cases, and how mixed block representations can be stale after substitution
- [#5809](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5809) (move Flambda2 export info to a section) prompted [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) to suggest making the section storage more generic to also accommodate Slambda code for layout poly
- [#5820](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5820) (use alloc_mode when building unknown types) received detailed review comments from [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) cautioning about the safety of pushing allocation modes from containers to children
- [#5662](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5662) (add `env_alloc_mode` to `Tpat_fun_layout`) has ongoing review discussion about the correct error message for the syntactic value restriction in layout-poly let bindings
- [#5663](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5663) (sort variable negative IDs in .cmi files) has a design discussion about whether to reuse or rename a helper function for constructing sort variables with explicit IDs
repositories.trends_summary: Work is converging on mixed block representation uniformity, layout polymorphism infrastructure, and the 5.4 runtime merge.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The representation of records and variants is being rationalised: all-value/void mixed records will become uniform blocks, float64 records will become mixed blocks, and legacy float-array representation will require an explicit opt-in attribute
- Layout polymorphism infrastructure is maturing with multiple PRs this week fixing sort variable level handling, instantiation errors, and generalisation correctness
- The Flambda2 reaper is being incrementally restructured, splitting `dep_solver.ml` into points-to analysis, unboxing analysis, and types rewrite modules
- Metaprogramming (quotation/splicing) is seeing incremental fixes to REPL usability and error reporting
- The SSA CFG variant draft opens exploration of loop optimisations (empty loop deletion) directly on the CFG IR
repositories.issues_summary: Two bugs were closed: a CSE regression with the n-way join algorithm and a type-directed disambiguation regression.
repositories.contributors_summary: Contributions came from across the team with particularly active work on layout poly, unboxed types, Flambda2, and the runtime.
