repositories.brief_summary: Merlin upgraded to OCaml 5.4 with expanded compiler flag coverage and improved ocamlopt test support.
repositories.new_features_summary: OCaml 5.4 upgrade with expanded ignored-flags lists and ocamlopt flag test coverage.
repositories.new_features:
- **OCaml 5.4 compatibility** upgrade by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923), bumping the opam dependency from `>= 5.2 & < 5.3` to `>= 5.4 & < 5.5`, removing the `base-effects` conflict, and reformatting the entire codebase with ocamlformat 0.29.0 ([#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227))
- Expanded **`ocaml_ignored_flags`** list in `mconfig.ml` to cover new OxCaml/flambda2 backend flags including `-clambda-checks`, `-dcfg`, `-dflambda-invariants`, `-enable-poll-insertion`, `-fno-asan`, `-function-sections`, `-gdwarf-may-alter-codegen-experimental`, `-internal-assembler`, `-long-frames`, `-verify-binary-emitter`, and many more ([#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227))
- Expanded **`ocaml_ignored_parametrized_flags`** in `mconfig.ml` to include new flags such as `-debug-long-frames-threshold`, `-flambda2-expert-max-function-simplify-run`, `-llvm-flags`, `-reaper-preserve-direct-calls`, and `-save-ir-after` ([#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227))
- Updated **magic number mapping** in `src/ocaml/typing/magic_numbers.ml` from revision 35 to 36, reflecting the new `Caml1999I576` identifier for 5.2.0minus-36 ([#231](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/231))
- Extended **compiler flags test** (`tests/test-dirs/ocaml-flags.t`) to cover both `ocamlopt.opt` and `ocamlc` flags (previously only `ocamlc` was tested), with improved error reporting that explains how to handle newly unrecognised flags ([#232](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/232))
repositories.activity_summary: All three merged PRs this week focus on keeping Merlin in sync with the OCaml 5.4 compiler.
repositories.activity:
- **OCaml 5.4 upgrade** merged by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) touching 62 files across analysis, kernel, frontend, and utility modules, with a separate formatting-only commit using ocamlformat 0.29.0 that reviewers were advised to skip ([#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227)); reviewed and approved by [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia)
- **Magic number update** merged without review by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) as a one-line trivial fix to keep the byte-code magic number consistent with the new compiler tag ([#231](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/231))
- **Compiler flags test gap** filled by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) — [#202](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/202) had added tests for `ocamlc` flags but omitted `ocamlopt`; [#232](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/232) adds the missing `ocamlopt.opt` coverage and improves the test's diagnostic output ([#232](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/232)), reviewed by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js)
repositories.trends_summary: Development this week is focused on tracking upstream OCaml compiler changes and maintaining flag-handling completeness.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Ongoing effort to keep Merlin's **compiler flag allowlist/ignorelist** in sync with OxCaml's extended flambda2 and LLVM backend flags, as evidenced by the large expansion of `ocaml_ignored_flags` and `ocaml_ignored_parametrized_flags` in [#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227) and [#232](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/232)
- Investment in **automated flag coverage tests** that detect when compiler flags are added without a corresponding Merlin entry, reducing the risk of silent breakage when OCaml is updated ([#232](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/232))
repositories.contributors_summary: Liam Stevenson authored all three merged PRs; Kostia Balytskyi and @dkalinichenko-js provided reviews.
repositories.contributors:
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) authored and merged all three PRs this week: the OCaml 5.4 upgrade ([#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227)), the magic number update ([#231](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/231)), and the compiler flags test extension ([#232](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/232))
- [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) reviewed and approved the OCaml 5.4 upgrade PR ([#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227)), noting a formatting style observation about `begin` placement
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) reviewed the compiler flags test PR and gave LGTM approval ([#232](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/232))

repositories.brief_summary: Discussion on open PR #36 to update the bootstrap compiler to OCaml 5.4.1 for OxCaml main branch.
repositories.activity_summary: PR #36 to add OCaml 5.4.1 as bootstrap compiler remains open with active design discussion.
repositories.activity: - **Bootstrap compiler update to 5.4.1** proposed by [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou) in [#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) (opened 2026-03-31, updated this week) to keep the `ocaml-variants` opam file in sync with OxCaml's main branch; the PR modifies 2 files with 22 additions and 19 deletions and is awaiting resolution of workflow concerns raised in review
repositories.discussions_summary: Technical discussion in PR #36 about how updating the bootstrap compiler interacts with existing opam switch workflows.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) raised concerns in [#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) that replacing the bootstrap compiler entry breaks the existing workflow for the `5.2.0minus-25` tag (which requires the `4.14.2` bootstrap), questioning how users would get a working version of `main` with this change
- [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou) clarified that the intended workflow relies on `opam compiler create oxcaml:main` (from [ocaml-opam/opam-compiler#42](https://github.com/ocaml-opam/opam-compiler/issues/42)), which would discover the `+ox` version and pin `ocaml-variants` with source from `main`; testing showed `main` builds successfully producing `5.2.0+ox`
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) noted the underlying issue that OxCaml lacks an `ocaml-variants.opam` file (historically introduced for OCaml 4.08/4.09 in [ocaml/ocaml#8604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/8604)) and flagged the related tracking issue [oxcaml/oxcaml#5344](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5344)
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing work to align OxCaml's opam packaging with upstream OCaml release cadence and tooling workflows.
repositories.emerging_trends: - The discussion in [#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) reflects a recurring challenge of keeping OxCaml's opam metadata synchronized with both the upstream OCaml release cycle and OxCaml-specific versioning schemes, with the `opam compiler` tool ([ocaml-opam/opam-compiler#42](https://github.com/ocaml-opam/opam-compiler/issues/42)) emerging as a key integration point for managing OxCaml switches
repositories.contributors_summary: Eduardo Souza opened PR #36 and responded to review; David Allsopp provided technical review feedback.
repositories.contributors:
- [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou) authored [#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) to update the bootstrap compiler to 5.4.1 and responded to review feedback explaining the intended `opam compiler` workflow
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) reviewed [#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36), raising workflow compatibility concerns and identifying the missing `ocaml-variants.opam` issue tracked in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5344](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5344)

group_summaries.brief_summary: Major releases (dune 3.22.2, merlin 5.7.0-504, ocaml-lsp 1.26.0) published to opam-repository alongside new tutorials and changelogs on ocaml.org.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New packages include ocaml-ai-sdk, yamlx, ezlua, and Chinese cryptography (sm); job listings and release changelogs added to ocaml.org.
group_summaries.new_features:
- New **ocaml-ai-sdk** (0.1) by [José Nogueira](https://github.com/thatportugueseguy): type-safe provider-agnostic AI SDK with Anthropic and OpenAI backends ([ocaml/opam-repository#29679](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29679))
- New **yamlx** (0.1.0) by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon): pure-OCaml YAML 1.2 parser with lossless comment-preserving AST, passes all 371 yaml-test-suite tests ([ocaml/opam-repository#29684](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29684))
- New **atd-jsonlike** and **atd-yamlx** packages as part of atd 4.1.0 by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon): generic JSON-like AST with source locations and YAML-to-ATD bridge ([ocaml/opam-repository#29709](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29709))
- New **ezlua** (0.1) by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube): OCaml-Lua codec runtime and high-level interpreter API ([ocaml/opam-repository#29655](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29655))
- New **sm** (0.1.1) by [sang](https://github.com/daysv): pure-OCaml Chinese National Cryptographic Algorithm Library (SM2/SM3/SM4/ZUC) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29698](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29698))
- New **jsonschema-core** and **jsonschema-validation** (0.1.0) by [Yuriy Krasilnikov](https://github.com/YuriyKrasilnikov): JSON Schema draft-07 validation with $ref resolution ([ocaml/opam-repository#29664](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29664))
- New **idna** (0.3.0) by [Yuriy Krasilnikov](https://github.com/YuriyKrasilnikov): pure-OCaml IDNA2008 hostname validation with Unicode 16.0.0 tables ([ocaml/opam-repository#29680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29680))
- New **layoutz** (0.1.0) by [Matthieu Court](https://github.com/mattlianje): Elm-style TUI runtime for terminal user interfaces ([ocaml/opam-repository#29665](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29665))
- New **snkv** (0.1.1) by [Tomasz Barański](https://github.com/tomob): OCaml FFI bindings for SNKV, a persistent ACID-compliant key-value store on SQLite's B-tree engine ([ocaml/opam-repository#29681](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29681))
- New **ephemeral** (1.0) by [Charles Averill](https://github.com/CharlesAverill): spacecraft trajectory visualization library ([ocaml/opam-repository#29693](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29693))
- New **tsdl-ttf** (0.7) by [珊瑚](https://github.com/sanette): SDL2_ttf bindings for use with Tsdl ([ocaml/opam-repository#29648](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29648))
- New **bin_there** and **ppx_bin_there** (0.1.12) by [David Teller](https://github.com/Yoric): serialization/deserialization with shared value support and PPX deriving ([ocaml/opam-repository#29677](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29677))
- **Release changelogs** for ocaml-lsp 1.26.0 and merlin 5.7.0-504 added to [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) by [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3605)), and dune 3.22.1 release announcement by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3596](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3596))
- New **job listings** added: Lead Systems Software Engineer at Cloud Software Group ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3600)) and Inria Rennes position on static analysis of OCaml programs by [Benoit Montagu](https://github.com/esope) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3601](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3601))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) saw major tooling releases including **dune** 3.22.2 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29704](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29704)), **merlin** 5.7.0-504 and **ocaml-lsp-server** 1.26.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29689](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29689)), and **atd** 4.1.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29709](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29709)), plus 12+ new package submissions
- [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) added release changelogs for the same tooling releases, two tutorial PRs for platform tooling (continuous benchmarking and OCaml-CI self-hosting), and multiple job listings and Planet posts
- Over 30 contributors were active across both repositories
- OCaml 4.14.3 compatibility constraints were applied to several packages in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) due to internal API changes ([ocaml/opam-repository#29692](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29692), [ocaml/opam-repository#29667](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29667))
- Windows MSVC build correctness was addressed through fixes to conf packages and a patched **camlidl** release ([ocaml/opam-repository#29691](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29691), [ocaml/opam-repository#29653](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29653))

- **Combobulate Emacs integration note** opened by [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) to document setting up combobulate for OCaml in Emacs ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3603))
- **Automated Planet scraping** added diverse community content across three PRs ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3597](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3597), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3602](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3602), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3604))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **merlin 5.7.0-504 and ocaml-lsp-server 1.26.0** were released to [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29689](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29689)) and their release changelogs were simultaneously added to [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) by [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3605))
- **dune** saw coordinated activity: a bug-fix 3.22.2 release landed in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29704](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29704)) while the preceding 3.22.1 release announcement was published on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3596](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3596)), with [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) contributing to both
- **textmate-language** 0.6.0 released to [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29706](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29706)) is a direct dependency of the **ochre** syntax highlighter submission ([ocaml/opam-repository#29700](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29700)) using TextMate grammars, with documentation relevance to the editor tooling coverage on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org)
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- OCaml 4.14.3 compatibility: multiple packages received incompatibility constraints due to removal of the `ce_rec` field in `Instruct.compilation_env`; js_of_ocaml-compiler and stdcompat were affected ([ocaml/opam-repository#29692](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29692), [ocaml/opam-repository#29667](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29667))
- **dune 3.22.1** reported as failing to build with OCaml 4.14.0 by [Ralf Jung](https://github.com/RalfJung); investigation determined the issue was user-environment specific and not a general regression ([ocaml/opam-repository#29658](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29658))
- **lwt6** constraints refinement PR opened by [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) with 84 changed files, awaiting CI results ([ocaml/opam-repository#29530](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29530))
- **Windows MSVC** build issues: mingw dependencies were incorrectly pulled in for MSVC builds of conf-cairo, conf-gtk3, and conf-pkg-config; reported by [Tõivo Leedjärv](https://github.com/tleedjarv) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29609](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29609))
- **conf-libffi** Windows fix by [Puneeth Chaganti](https://github.com/punchagan): added mingw-w64-shims as direct build dependency to prevent dependency ordering issues ([ocaml/opam-repository#29691](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29691))
- **camlidl** Windows patch by [Boris Yakobowski](https://github.com/yakobowski): fixed Windows Makefile extension and GCC 14 build failures, establishing policy of using patch versions (e.g. 1.13-1) for patched packages ([ocaml/opam-repository#29653](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29653))
- **piqi** build failures: [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) added availability constraints to fix C binding issues with newer compilers ([ocaml/opam-repository#29701](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29701))
group_summaries.emerging_trends:

- **Chinese national cryptographic standards** (SM2/SM3/SM4/ZUC) now available as a pure OCaml library (**sm**), expanding cryptographic algorithm coverage ([ocaml/opam-repository#29698](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29698))

- **Platform tutorial expansion** on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org): [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac) opened two new tutorials covering continuous benchmarking with current-bench ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3598](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3598)) and OCaml-CI self-hosting ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3599](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3599))
- **Editor tooling documentation** expanding with coverage of combobulate Emacs integration ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3603)) following upstream merge of OCaml support in combobulate
- **OCaml 4.14.3 compatibility** work ongoing across the ecosystem, with multiple packages receiving constraint updates due to internal OCaml API changes ([ocaml/opam-repository#29692](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29692), [ocaml/opam-repository#29667](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29667))
- **Windows MSVC build** support improvements continuing, with several conf packages fixed to avoid incorrect mingw dependency pulls ([ocaml/opam-repository#29691](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29691), [ocaml/opam-repository#29653](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29653))
- **Multimedia streaming ecosystem** active: both **liquidsoap** and **ocaml-ffmpeg** released new versions in the same week ([ocaml/opam-repository#29708](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29708), [ocaml/opam-repository#29703](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29703))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Dune 3.22.2, merlin v5.7.0-504, and ocaml-lsp 1.26.0 released; js_of_ocaml Wasm dynlink/toplevel support merged.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune adds watch-mode tracing and CLI tools; ocaml-lsp 1.26.0 ships refactorExtract and rangeFormatting; js_of_ocaml Wasm dynlink lands.
group_summaries.new_features:
- [ocaml/dune#14163](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14163) adds trace events for watch-mode iterations (build start, restart, finish), enabling profiling of incremental builds, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [ocaml/dune#14083](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14083) introduces `dune init start-file` to scaffold the recommended entry point for driving dune in watch mode, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [ocaml/dune#14068](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14068) adds `dune internal sexp-pp`, a command to pretty-print s-expressions for debugging dune files, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [ocaml/dune#14071](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14071) adds a `--deps` flag to `dune rules` to print rule dependencies directly, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [ocaml/dune#14073](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14073) adds the ability to dump the digest database for debugging stale-dependency investigations, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [ocaml/dune#13997](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13997) adds an `extracted_files` field to the `rocq.extraction` stanza, preventing extracted non-OCaml files from being wiped on subsequent builds, by [Will Thomas](https://github.com/Durbatuluk1701)
- [ocaml/dune#14059](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14059) extends the copy action to support directory targets, a prerequisite for Melange runtime asset promotion, by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
- [ocaml/merlin#1948](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1948) introduces the new experimental `refactor-extract-region` command for extracting a code region into a fresh `let` binding, released in [v5.7.0-504](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/releases/tag/v5.7.0-504)
- [ocaml/merlin#2025](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2025) adds an `"Other"` variant to `locate-types` results; [ocaml/merlin#2027](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2027) omits `option` for optional parameters; [ocaml/merlin#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029) adds `-end-position` to the `enclosing` command
- Signature help fixes in merlin: no longer loops after completion ([ocaml/merlin#2023](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2023)), labelled/optional parameter handling ([ocaml/merlin#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032)), and `in`-keyword trigger fix ([ocaml/merlin#2036](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2036))
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1545) adds `ocamllsp/refactorExtract` custom LSP request; [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583) adds `destruct`; [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584) adds `locate_types`
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1591](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1591) adds standard `textDocument/rangeFormatting` support, enabling format-on-selection in editors
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1600) adds `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM` and `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_ROOT` environment variables for alternative build system support via `merlin-dot-protocol`, breaking the previous assumption that only Dune projects are supported
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187) merges Wasm dynlink and toplevel support into `wasm_of_ocaml`: loads `.wasmo`/`.wasma` files, supports `Dynlink.loadfile`, builds interactive toplevels, includes virtual filesystem, Graphics primitives, and new `--dynlink`, `--toplevel`, `--no-cmis`, `--export` CLI flags, by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **ocaml/dune**: Released 3.22.2; added watch-mode tracing, new CLI tools (`dune init start-file` via [ocaml/dune#14083](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14083), `dune internal sexp-pp` via [ocaml/dune#14068](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14068)), and continued RPC/scheduler library consolidation ([ocaml/dune#14147](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14147), [ocaml/dune#14145](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14145), [ocaml/dune#14153](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14153), [ocaml/dune#14155](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14155))
- **ocaml/merlin**: Released v5.7.0-504 with new `refactor-extract-region` command ([ocaml/merlin#1948](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1948)), `locate-types` and `enclosing` improvements, and multiple signature help fixes; two open PRs target ocaml-index memory bounding ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)) and on-disk index pointing ([ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051))
- **ocaml/ocaml-lsp**: Released 1.26.0 with `refactorExtract` ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1545)), `destruct` ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583)), `locate_types` ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584)) custom requests, `rangeFormatting` ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1591](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1591)), and alternative build system support ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1600))
- **ocaml/opam**: Three new PRs opened — GC corruption fix in the `uname` FFI binding ([ocaml/opam#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880)), removal of OCaml 4.08–4.10 build support ([ocaml/opam#6879](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6879)), and dependency lower-bound cleanup ([ocaml/opam#6878](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6878)); expired PGP signing key resolved via [ocaml/opam#6877](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6877)
- **ocaml/odoc**: Proof-of-concept `@inline` tag for module declarations ([ocaml/odoc#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408)) opened and closed after discussion; OCaml 5.5 compatibility PR ([ocaml/odoc#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406)) marked ready for review; OxCaml PR ([ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399)) addressing review feedback
- **ocsigen/js_of_ocaml**: Merged Wasm dynlink and toplevel support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)) after development since February (6383 additions); two follow-up Wasm size-reduction PRs opened immediately after ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212))
- **ocaml/ocamlfind**, **ocaml/opam-file-format**, **ocaml-dune/spawn**: No recorded activity this week
group_summaries.activity_summary: RPC/scheduler consolidation in Dune, ocaml-index improvements in Merlin, opam GC and build cleanup, odoc OCaml 5.5 and OxCaml compatibility work.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) landed multiple ocaml/dune package consolidation PRs: [ocaml/dune#14147](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14147) merges `csexp_rpc` into `rpc`, [ocaml/dune#14145](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14145) merges `dune_rpc_client`/`dune_rpc_server`, [ocaml/dune#14153](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14153) merges `fsevents` into `dune_scheduler`, [ocaml/dune#14155](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14155) merges `fswatch_win` into `dune_scheduler`
- [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) is developing per-module inter-library dependency filtering in [ocaml/dune#14116](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14116), using ocamldep output to eliminate unnecessary recompilation when an unused module in a dependency changes
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) proposes an `(archived false)` field on library stanzas in [ocaml/dune#14144](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14144) to allow linking library objects directly without generating `.cma`/`.cmxa` archives
- [Ambre Austen Suhamy](https://github.com/ElectreAAS) continues work on resolving directory symlinks in fetched package sources in [ocaml/dune#13792](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13792) to avoid build sandbox breakage
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) managed the Dune 3.22.2 release process ([ocaml/dune#14112](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14112)) and continues work on release automation in [ocaml/dune#14115](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14115)
- [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) landed opam packaging checks in Dune CI via [ocaml/dune#14078](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14078), catching `opam lint` failures on `.opam` files on every relevant PR
- [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) is developing support for pointing indexes in ocaml-index in [ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051), with review from [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) and [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) on store ID validation, error reporting, and path handling
- [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) opened [ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050) replacing an unbounded `Hashtbl` with an LRU cache in the ocaml-index component to bound memory growth
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) marked [ocaml/odoc#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) ready for review, adding support for OCaml 5.5 external types (rendered as abstract) and module arrows
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) addressed review feedback on [ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399) for upstream OxCaml support, removing an unnecessary type annotation flagged by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam)
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened [ocaml/opam#6879](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6879) to remove OCaml 4.08–4.10 build support and [ocaml/opam#6878](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6878) for dependency lower-bound cleanup; also updated the action-reordering draft [ocaml/opam#6864](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6864) with a PoC differentiating installs, upgrades, and removals
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened [ocaml/opam#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880) fixing GC root registration in the `uname` C stub to prevent potential memory corruption
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) opened two Wasm optimization PRs in ocsigen/js_of_ocaml: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211) puts constant expressions into global variables to reduce toplevel function size (halving it for the PRT benchmark), and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212) extracts loops from the toplevel function into helpers to avoid wasteful Wasm engine tier-up
- [Gaëtan Gilbert](https://github.com/SkySkimmer) pinged maintainers for review of [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1579), a correctness fix for `-short-paths` config propagation in Merlin that has been open since December 2025
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Merlin → ocaml-lsp feature pipeline**: The new `refactor-extract-region` command in merlin v5.7.0-504 ([ocaml/merlin#1948](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1948)) is directly exposed as `ocamllsp/refactorExtract` in ocaml-lsp 1.26.0 ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1545)); merlin's `destruct` and `locate-types` improvements similarly flow into new ocaml-lsp custom requests ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583), [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584))
- **Coordinated merlin/ocaml-lsp release**: Both merlin v5.7.0-504 and ocaml-lsp 1.26.0 were released by the same team ([PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) and [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos)) in the same week; [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) noted on [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1600) that merlin-lib version constraints needed updating before the lsp release could finalize
- **ocaml-lsp build system agnosticism via merlin-dot-protocol**: [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1600) introduces `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM` support, allowing ocaml-lsp to obtain Merlin configuration from non-Dune build systems that implement the `merlin-dot-protocol` interface, breaking a long-standing Dune-only assumption
- **[@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) active across merlin and odoc**: provided detailed review on [ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051) covering store ID validation and path handling, and worked on [ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399) for OxCaml upstream support
- **Dune opam infrastructure and opam packaging quality**: Dune [ocaml/dune#14108](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14108) updates `.opam` file generation to use `diff` actions for better CI coverage, while [ocaml/opam#6878](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6878) adds missing lower bounds across opam build dependencies — both addressing packaging quality across the toolchain
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [ocaml/dune#14121](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14121) outlines the long-term roadmap for `dune monitor`: moving to a separate binary, replacing Fiber with Lwt, unvendoring Notty/Lwd, and communicating over RPC and trace files, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- [ocaml/dune#14112](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14112) documents the 3.22.2 release process; [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) clarified the policy of restricting patch releases to regressions only to maintain release cadence
- [ocaml/dune#13682](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13682) on adding `(excluded_modules)` was closed after [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) and [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) agreed on a broader `(archived false)` direction, leading to [ocaml/dune#14144](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14144)
- [ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051) design discussion: [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) raised placement of `id` field in store type for better semantic clarity; [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) proposed an `Outdated_store of [ \`Missing_file of string | \`Index_ids_do_not_match ]` exception type for outdated build error reporting; debate on relative vs absolute path handling in ocaml-index
- [ocaml/odoc#564](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/564) saw renewed discussion sparked by [ocaml/odoc#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408): [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) argued that user-controlled module inlining breaks predictable URL structures; [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) suggested sane defaults with opt-in overrides
- [ocaml/opam#6877](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6877): expired PGP signing key reported by [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu), triaged by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) linking the renewal work at [ocaml-opam/opam2web#254](https://github.com/ocaml-opam/opam2web/issues/254), and confirmed resolved by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- [ocaml/opam#6875](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6875): [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) raised outdated opam versions in DockerHub `ocaml/opam` images; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) clarified those images are maintained by [ocurrent/docker-base-images](https://github.com/ocurrent/docker-base-images/issues), not the opam team
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212): [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) raised whether extracting loops from toplevel functions could benefit the JS backend as well as Wasm, as Wasm/JS engines could independently optimize extracted helpers
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Coordinated toolchain releases**: Dune 3.22.2, merlin v5.7.0-504, and ocaml-lsp 1.26.0 all released in the same week by overlapping teams, reflecting synchronized toolchain updates across interrelated projects
- **Internal library consolidation in Dune**: [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) is systematically merging previously separate packages into their natural homes ([ocaml/dune#14147](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14147), [ocaml/dune#14145](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14145), [ocaml/dune#14153](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14153), [ocaml/dune#14155](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14155)), removing abstraction layers that added complexity without benefit
- **Build tracing expansion in Dune**: lock-dir load events ([ocaml/dune#14064](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14064)), watch-mode iteration events ([ocaml/dune#14163](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14163)), and digest database dumps ([ocaml/dune#14073](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14073)) all landed in the same week, indicating a sustained effort to improve build observability
- **Expanding LSP custom request surface in ocaml-lsp**: `refactorExtract`, `destruct`, and `locate_types` join the existing set of extensions ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1545), [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583), [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584)), reflecting a deliberate effort to expose Merlin capabilities via LSP
- **ocaml-index scalability focus in Merlin**: concurrent PRs for LRU-based memory bounding ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)) and support for pointing to existing on-disk index files ([ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)) suggest continued investment in scaling the indexing infrastructure
- **Wasm backend capability parity in js_of_ocaml**: the merged [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187) closes the dynlink/toplevel gap; the immediate follow-up PRs [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211) and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212) show active attention to the size and performance costs of new features
- **Build tooling debt reduction in opam**: [ocaml/opam#6879](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6879) drops OCaml 4.08–4.10 build support and [ocaml/opam#6878](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6878) tightens dependency lower bounds, continuing a pattern of narrowing the supported build surface
- **GC safety in C stubs**: the `uname` FFI fix in [ocaml/opam#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880) is consistent with earlier GC root registration fixes, suggesting ongoing attention to FFI correctness in opam's C bindings

group_summaries.brief_summary: oxcaml/oxcaml saw broad progress on layout poly, unboxed types, and Flambda2; Merlin upgraded to OCaml 5.4.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Runtime 5 now default, custom or_null types, layout-poly AST nodes, reversible name mangling, and Merlin's 5.4 upgrade landed.
group_summaries.new_features:
- Runtime 5 is now the default in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5780](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5780)
- Custom user-defined `[@@or_null]` declarations with custom constructor names now supported, generalising the built-in `Null`/`This` representation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5668](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5668))
- `Texp_apply_layout` node added to the typed AST for layout-polymorphic function instantiation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5634](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5634))
- New reversible name mangling scheme added, enabling full module path reconstruction from mangled symbols ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5099](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5099))
- Small integer shift intrinsics added to the backend ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5814](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5814))
- `-Ix` flag added for include paths where `.cmx` files are guaranteed available, threading `cmx_guaranteed` through the persistent env ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5654](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5654))
- n-way join now creates extra params for variables produced during the join, improving CSE precision ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5751](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5751))
- Bigarray construction routines now return `@ unique` values ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5767](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5767))
- Tick thread moved into the runtime ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5349](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5349))
- Prologue shrink-wrap enabled by default ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5762](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5762))
- `SPLIT_AROUND_LOOPS` regalloc parameter default flipped ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5761](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5761))
- Upgraded to ocamlformat 0.29.0 in both [oxcaml/oxcaml#5777](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5777) and [oxcaml/merlin#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227)
- **Merlin OCaml 5.4 compatibility**: opam dependency bumped to `>= 5.4 & < 5.5`, `base-effects` conflict removed, codebase reformatted with ocamlformat 0.29.0 ([oxcaml/merlin#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227))
- Expanded `ocaml_ignored_flags` in Merlin to cover new OxCaml/flambda2 backend flags ([oxcaml/merlin#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227))
- Updated magic number mapping in Merlin to revision 36 (`Caml1999I576` for 5.2.0minus-36) ([oxcaml/merlin#231](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/231))
- Extended Merlin compiler flags test to cover `ocamlopt.opt` in addition to `ocamlc` ([oxcaml/merlin#232](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/232))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) was the most active repository with over 30 PRs merged, spanning layout polymorphism infrastructure, unboxed/mixed-block type representations, Flambda2 reaper refactoring, backend codegen, and runtime improvements
- [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) completed its OCaml 5.4 upgrade ([#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227)), updated its magic number mapping ([#231](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/231)), and extended compiler-flag test coverage ([#232](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/232))
- [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) has an open PR ([#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36)) to update the bootstrap compiler to OCaml 5.4.1, pending resolution of workflow compatibility concerns
- [janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository) has a single open maintenance PR ([#75](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository/issues/75)) to update the OxCaml opam-repository pointer away from a deprecated branch
- [oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml) had no recorded activity this week
group_summaries.activity_summary: Flambda2 reaper restructuring, unboxed record/variant representation overhaul, layout poly correctness fixes, and runtime/linker improvements dominated oxcaml/oxcaml activity.
group_summaries.activity:
- Draft SSA variant of the CFG backend opened by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5810](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5810)), with a follow-up draft for empty loop deletion using induction variable analysis opened by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5840](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5840))
- Represent all-value/void mixed records and variants as uniform blocks proposed by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5774](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5774))
- Represent `float64` records as mixed blocks with `[@@represent_as_float_array]` attribute proposed by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5796](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5796))
- Require `[@@flatten_floats]` attribute on mixed `float`-`float64` records proposed by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5795](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5795))
- Skip `caml_modify` for `non_pointer` values opened by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), building on earlier work by [Zack Eisbach](https://github.com/zackbach) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5831](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5831))
- Move Flambda2 export info out of `.cmx` inline `Marshal` block into a section opened by [Nicolas Trangez](https://github.com/NicolasT) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5809](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5809))
- Structured Fexpr primitive representation opened by [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5825](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5825))
- Reaper code size computation when rebuilding terms opened by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5793](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5793))
- `dep_solver.ml` split into separate modules merged by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5776](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5776)); follow-up cleanup merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5787](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5787)); `field_{usages,sources}_top` removed ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5748](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5748))
- `lpoly` generalise simplified via path compression by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5792](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5792))
- Layout instantiation restricted to static values ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5783](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5783)); layout var instantiation error changed to `fatal_error` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5817](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5817)); `moregeneral` always restores `current_level` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5816](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5816))
- Scannable axes compute nullability and separability merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5169](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5169))
- Tick thread reset after `fork()` merged by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5829](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5829))
- Fine-grained profiling added to the linker scan-file loop by [Nicolas Trangez](https://github.com/NicolasT) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5808](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5808))
- Avoid persistent env wrapper partial applications, saving ~100 MB allocation when compiling `typecore.ml` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5631](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5631))
- Patricia trees: `union_total` function families added to avoid allocations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5803](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5803)); callback modules for function specialisation merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5770](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5770))
- Eliminate useless shifts in string/bytes indexed access ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5746](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5746))
- Fix metaprogramming REPL syntax directive issue by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5801](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5801)); graceful reporting of unsupported language features in runtime metaprogramming merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5823](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5823))
- Handle staging in `Printtyp` more carefully by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5785](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5785))
- 5.4 merge: various testsuite promotions merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5757](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5757))
- Bootstrap compiler update to OCaml 5.4.1 in [oxcaml/opam-repository#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) remains open pending resolution of workflow concerns raised by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)
- OxCaml opam-repository pointer update away from deprecated branch proposed by [Xiaomin Liu](https://github.com/xl4624) in [janestreet/opam-repository#75](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository/issues/75)
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- The OCaml 5.4 upgrade spans both [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) (Runtime 5 as default at [oxcaml/oxcaml#5780](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5780), 5.4 testsuite merge at [oxcaml/oxcaml#5757](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5757)) and [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) (5.4 opam dependency bump at [oxcaml/merlin#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227))
- The ocamlformat 0.29.0 upgrade was applied in coordination: [oxcaml/oxcaml#5777](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5777) and [oxcaml/merlin#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227) both reformatted their codebases in the same week
- The bootstrap compiler update in [oxcaml/opam-repository#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) (targeting OCaml 5.4.1) is directly related to the 5.4 merge activity in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s review of [oxcaml/opam-repository#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) identified a missing `ocaml-variants.opam` file tracked as [oxcaml/oxcaml#5344](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5344), linking opam packaging concerns back to the main compiler repository
- [janestreet/opam-repository#75](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository/issues/75) proposes updating the pointer to [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) away from a deprecated branch, connecting the two opam repositories
- Merlin's expanded `ocaml_ignored_flags` list in [oxcaml/merlin#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227) directly reflects new compiler flags introduced in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) (flambda2, LLVM backend, internal assembler flags)
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5831](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5831) (skip `caml_modify` for `non_pointer` values) includes detailed discussion of interactions with abstract kinds, type substitution edge cases, and how mixed block representations can become stale after substitution
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5809](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5809) (move Flambda2 export info to a section) prompted [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) to suggest making section storage more generic to also accommodate Slambda code for layout poly
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5820](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5820) (use alloc_mode when building unknown types) received detailed review from [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) cautioning about the safety of pushing allocation modes from containers to children
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5662](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5662) (add `env_alloc_mode` to `Tpat_fun_layout`) has ongoing review discussion about the correct error message for the syntactic value restriction in layout-poly let bindings
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5663](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5663) (sort variable negative IDs in .cmi files) has a design discussion about whether to reuse or rename a helper function for constructing sort variables with explicit IDs
- [oxcaml/opam-repository#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36): [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) raised that replacing the bootstrap compiler entry breaks the existing workflow for the `5.2.0minus-25` tag; [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou) clarified the intended workflow via `opam compiler create oxcaml:main` ([ocaml-opam/opam-compiler#42](https://github.com/ocaml-opam/opam-compiler/issues/42))
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- The representation of unboxed records and variants is being systematically rationalised: all-value/void mixed records will become uniform blocks ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5774](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5774)), `float64` records will become mixed blocks ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5796](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5796)), and legacy float-array representation will require an explicit opt-in ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5795](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5795))
- Layout polymorphism infrastructure is maturing with multiple PRs fixing sort variable level handling ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5791](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5791)), instantiation errors ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5817](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5817)), and generalisation correctness ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5792](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5792))
- The Flambda2 reaper is being incrementally restructured: `dep_solver.ml` split into points-to analysis, unboxing analysis, and types rewrite modules ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5776](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5776)), with code size computation next ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5793](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5793))
- The SSA CFG variant draft ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5810](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5810)) opens a path to loop-level optimisations such as empty loop deletion ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5840](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5840)) directly on the CFG IR
- Metaprogramming (quotation/splicing) is seeing incremental fixes to REPL usability ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5801](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5801)) and error reporting ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5823](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5823))
- Ongoing effort to keep Merlin's compiler flag ignore-lists in sync with OxCaml's extended flambda2 and LLVM backend flags, now reinforced by automated `ocamlopt`-coverage tests ([oxcaml/merlin#232](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/232))
- OxCaml opam packaging coordination is an active area, with both [oxcaml/opam-repository#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) and [janestreet/opam-repository#75](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository/issues/75) open this week to align repository pointers and bootstrap compiler versions with the 5.4 compiler branch

group_summaries.brief_summary: Keep-expansion typechecker fix and primitive aliases (RFC #44) merged; implicit source positions RFC design narrowed to options 2a/2b.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Primitive aliases (RFC #44), Atomic.update, Format.pp_print_iarray/dynarray landed; ocamltest cold predicate added.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Primitive aliases** (implementing [ocaml/RFCs#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44)) landed via [ocaml/ocaml#13377](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13377), enabling `external foo = Bar.baz` syntax to alias C primitives; contributed by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke), reviewed by [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop), [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25), and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
- **`Atomic.update`** was merged via [ocaml/ocaml#10798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10798), adding a retry-loop helper `('a -> 'a) -> 'a t -> unit` to `Atomic`, `Atomic.Loc`, and `Atomic.Array`; contributed by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) with extensive review.
- **`Format.pp_print_iarray`** was added via [ocaml/ocaml#14724](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14724) by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), addressing the stdlib gap noted in [ocaml/ocaml#14320](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14320).
- **`Format.pp_print_dynarray`** was added via [ocaml/ocaml#14726](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14726) by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), building on the iarray printer.
- **ocamltest `cold` predicate** was introduced via [ocaml/ocaml#14713](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14713) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), allowing tests to be disabled by default and run only when explicitly requested, closing [ocaml/ocaml#12418](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12418).
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity was concentrated in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml), with a high-impact week combining long-awaited typechecker corrections, runtime GC fixes, and stdlib additions.
- The "keep expansion" typechecker overhaul [ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) merged after years of development, resolving multiple non-termination and principality bugs.
- Primitive aliases from [ocaml/RFCs#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44) were implemented and merged via [ocaml/ocaml#13377](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13377), completing a full RFC-to-implementation cycle.
- In [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs), design discussion on the implicit source positions RFC ([ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)) converged toward implementation options 2a/2b.
- Key contributors active across both repositories include [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole).
group_summaries.activity_summary: Keep-expansion typechecker merged, ephemeron GC bugs fixed, runtime and stdlib improved, and RFC #52 design converged.
group_summaries.activity:
- **Keep expansion** typechecker overhaul [ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) was merged after years of development by [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) and [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), fixing non-termination bugs ([ocaml/ocaml#9314](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/9314), [ocaml/ocaml#13230](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13230), [ocaml/ocaml#13881](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13881), [ocaml/ocaml#14036](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14036)) and principality regressions.
- **Ephemeron orphaning bug fix** for 5.5 was submitted and merged via [ocaml/ocaml#14718](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14718) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), addressing runtime assertion failures reported in [ocaml/ocaml#14349](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14349).
- A series of **ephemeron runtime refactoring PRs** ([ocaml/ocaml#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732), [ocaml/ocaml#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733), [ocaml/ocaml#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734), [ocaml/ocaml#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735)) were opened by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) to clarify phase-change logic and fix the correctness bug on trunk.
- **Domain unique_id assignment** was moved earlier during domain creation via [ocaml/ocaml#14716](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14716) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), improving debuggability of domain lifecycle logging.
- **GADT scope-escape hint** was added to typechecker error messages via [ocaml/ocaml#14695](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14695) by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), providing guidance for users encountering ambivalent type errors.
- **Typechecker memory usage fix** for counter-example search was proposed via [ocaml/ocaml#14727](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14727) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), addressing excessive memory use in pattern-matching exhaustiveness checks.
- **Compaction corner case test** was improved via [ocaml/ocaml#14685](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14685) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), reducing test runtime from 14s to 1.5s while reproducing the original bug more reliably.
- **`translmod.ml` refactoring** introducing a `lambda_subst` helper was merged via [ocaml/ocaml#14728](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14728) by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb).
- **Regression test for rectypes keep-expansion** was merged via [ocaml/ocaml#14730](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14730) by [Brandon Stride](https://github.com/brandonzstride), preventing re-introduction of the typechecker infinite loop.
- **Minor cleanup in `Typecore.type_effect_cases`** was merged via [ocaml/ocaml#14723](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14723) by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin).
- **Implicit source positions** RFC ([ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)) design discussion progressed, with [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) converging on options 2a/2b as the path forward.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Primitive aliases RFC #44 → implementation**: [ocaml/RFCs#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44) completed its full RFC-to-merge cycle this week, with the implementation landing in [ocaml/ocaml#13377](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13377); [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) authored the implementation after [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop), [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25), and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) completed review.
- **Implicit source positions RFC #52 → future ocaml/ocaml work**: design discussion in [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) between [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) narrowed the implementation strategy to options 2a/2b, with 2a identified as an acceptable incremental first step toward the full 2b approach before work moves to [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml).
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) contributed actively to both repositories this week, with Scherer driving runtime/stdlib merges in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) ([ocaml/ocaml#14716](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14716), [ocaml/ocaml#14718](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14718), [ocaml/ocaml#10798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10798)) and RFC design in [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52).
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Ephemeron orphaning and adoption semantics** were analysed in [ocaml/ocaml#14720](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14720) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), questioning whether orphaned ephemerons should survive across GC cycles and proposing simplifications; the thread involves [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez), [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk), [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes), and [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan).
- **Adding extension points to the AST** for ppxlib compatibility was discussed in [ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668) by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb), exploring how to encode new language features without breaking existing ppx-es, with a concrete proposal forthcoming.
- **Misleading syntactic arity error message** was reported in [ocaml/ocaml#14719](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14719) by [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop) for valid functions with module-dependent arrows, prompting a fix PR [ocaml/ocaml#14721](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14721) from [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron).
- **`Atomic.fold_update` naming** continued in [ocaml/ocaml#14725](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14725), with [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) and [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) preferring `modify_get` or `map_update` over `fold_update`.
- **Stdlib gaps around `iarray`** were noted in [ocaml/ocaml#14320](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14320) by [Andreas Rossberg](https://github.com/rossberg), flagging that `Iarray.sub` has a divergent signature and `Dynarray.to/of_iarray` conversions are absent, with [ocaml/ocaml#14724](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14724) as a partial fix.
- **Implicit source positions implementation strategy** in [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52): [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) agreed that option 2a is a viable incremental path toward the fuller 2b design for the `[%call_pos]` feature.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Typechecker correctness and well-foundedness**: the landmark keep-expansion merge [ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) resolves deep-seated non-termination and principality bugs; follow-on work is expected around class types and recursive modules.
- **Ephemeron GC hardening for multicore**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened a structured multi-PR series ([ocaml/ocaml#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732)–[ocaml/ocaml#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735)) to systematically clarify and fix ephemeron lifecycle management, motivated by multicoretests reproductions from [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) in [ocaml/ocaml#14349](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14349).
- **Stdlib completeness for immutable and dynamic arrays**: addition of `pp_print_iarray` [ocaml/ocaml#14724](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14724) and `pp_print_dynarray` [ocaml/ocaml#14726](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14726) reflects ongoing work to bring `iarray` and `Dynarray` to feature parity with `Array`, with further gaps tracked in [ocaml/ocaml#14320](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14320).
- **RFC-to-implementation pipeline**: primitive aliases completing the [ocaml/RFCs#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44) → [ocaml/ocaml#13377](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13377) cycle, and implicit source positions ([ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)) advancing toward implementation, illustrate the RFC process maturing as a design gateway for language changes.
- **CI security hygiene**: [ocaml/ocaml#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14676) by [Austin Theriault](https://github.com/ajbt200128) proposed preventing shell injection in GitHub Actions workflows, reflecting broader supply-chain security attention.

weekly_summary.brief_summary: Dune 3.22.2, merlin v5.7.0-504, and ocaml-lsp 1.26.0 released; OCaml keep-expansion typechecker merged; OxCaml sets Runtime 5 as default.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: Primitive aliases, Atomic.update, LSP refactorExtract and rangeFormatting, Wasm dynlink, and ocaml-ai-sdk and yamlx packages added.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Primitive aliases** (implementing [ocaml/RFCs#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44)) landed via [ocaml/ocaml#13377](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13377), enabling `external foo = Bar.baz` syntax to alias C primitives; contributed by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke), reviewed by [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop), [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25), and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Atomic.update`** merged via [ocaml/ocaml#10798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10798), adding a retry-loop helper `('a -> 'a) -> 'a t -> unit` to `Atomic`, `Atomic.Loc`, and `Atomic.Array`; contributed by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Format.pp_print_iarray`** added via [ocaml/ocaml#14724](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14724) and **`Format.pp_print_dynarray`** via [ocaml/ocaml#14726](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14726), both by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), filling stdlib gaps noted in [ocaml/ocaml#14320](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14320)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **ocamltest `cold` predicate** introduced via [ocaml/ocaml#14713](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14713) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), allowing tests to be disabled by default and run only when explicitly requested, closing [ocaml/ocaml#12418](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12418)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Runtime 5 is now the default** in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5780](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5780)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Custom user-defined `[@@or_null]` declarations** with custom constructor names now supported, generalising the built-in `Null`/`This` representation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5668](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5668))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`Texp_apply_layout` node** added to the typed AST for layout-polymorphic function instantiation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5634](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5634))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **New reversible name mangling scheme** added, enabling full module path reconstruction from mangled symbols ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5099](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5099))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`-Ix` flag** added for include paths where `.cmx` files are guaranteed available, threading `cmx_guaranteed` through the persistent env ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5654](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5654))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Small integer shift intrinsics** added to the backend ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5814](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5814)); **prologue shrink-wrap enabled by default** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5762](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5762))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Bigarray construction routines** now return `@ unique` values ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5767](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5767))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Merlin OCaml 5.4 compatibility**: opam dependency bumped to `>= 5.4 & < 5.5`, `base-effects` conflict removed, and `ocaml_ignored_flags` expanded for new OxCaml backend flags ([oxcaml/merlin#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune watch-mode tracing** adds trace events for watch-mode iterations via [ocaml/dune#14163](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14163), enabling profiling of incremental builds, by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`dune init start-file`** scaffolds the recommended entry point for driving dune in watch mode ([ocaml/dune#14083](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14083)); **`dune internal sexp-pp`** pretty-prints s-expressions for debugging dune files ([ocaml/dune#14068](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14068)); **`--deps` flag to `dune rules`** prints rule dependencies directly ([ocaml/dune#14071](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14071)); all by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune `extracted_files` field** in the `rocq.extraction` stanza prevents extracted non-OCaml files from being wiped on subsequent builds ([ocaml/dune#13997](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13997))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune copy action** extended to support directory targets, a prerequisite for Melange runtime asset promotion ([ocaml/dune#14059](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14059))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Merlin `refactor-extract-region`** experimental command for extracting a code region into a fresh `let` binding, released in [v5.7.0-504](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/releases/tag/v5.7.0-504) via [ocaml/merlin#1948](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1948)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Merlin enhancements**: `"Other"` variant in `locate-types` results ([ocaml/merlin#2025](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2025)), `-end-position` to `enclosing` command ([ocaml/merlin#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029)), and multiple signature help fixes ([ocaml/merlin#2023](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2023), [ocaml/merlin#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032), [ocaml/merlin#2036](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2036))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **ocaml-lsp 1.26.0** ships `ocamllsp/refactorExtract` ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1545)), `destruct` ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1583)), and `locate_types` ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584)) custom LSP requests
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`textDocument/rangeFormatting`** standard LSP support added to ocaml-lsp, enabling format-on-selection in editors ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1591](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1591))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **ocaml-lsp build system agnosticism** via `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_BUILD_SYSTEM` and `$OCAMLLSP_PROJECT_ROOT` environment variables, breaking the previous Dune-only assumption ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1600))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Wasm dynlink and toplevel support** merged into `wasm_of_ocaml` after development since February: loads `.wasmo`/`.wasma` files, supports `Dynlink.loadfile`, builds interactive toplevels, and adds `--dynlink`, `--toplevel`, `--no-cmis`, `--export` CLI flags ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ New **ocaml-ai-sdk** (0.1) by [José Nogueira](https://github.com/thatportugueseguy): type-safe provider-agnostic AI SDK with Anthropic and OpenAI backends ([ocaml/opam-repository#29679](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29679))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ New **yamlx** (0.1.0) by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon): pure-OCaml YAML 1.2 parser with lossless comment-preserving AST, passes all 371 yaml-test-suite tests ([ocaml/opam-repository#29684](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29684))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ New **ezlua** (0.1) by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube): OCaml-Lua codec runtime and high-level interpreter API ([ocaml/opam-repository#29655](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29655))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ New **sm** (0.1.1) by [sang](https://github.com/daysv): pure-OCaml Chinese National Cryptographic Algorithm Library (SM2/SM3/SM4/ZUC) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29698](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29698))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ New **jsonschema-core** and **jsonschema-validation** (0.1.0) by [Yuriy Krasilnikov](https://github.com/YuriyKrasilnikov): JSON Schema draft-07 validation with $ref resolution ([ocaml/opam-repository#29664](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29664)); new **idna** (0.3.0): pure-OCaml IDNA2008 hostname validation ([ocaml/opam-repository#29680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29680))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ New **layoutz** (0.1.0) by [Matthieu Court](https://github.com/mattlianje): Elm-style TUI runtime for terminal user interfaces ([ocaml/opam-repository#29665](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29665)); new **snkv** (0.1.1) by [Tomasz Barański](https://github.com/tomob): OCaml FFI bindings for a persistent ACID-compliant SQLite key-value store ([ocaml/opam-repository#29681](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29681))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ New **atd-jsonlike** and **atd-yamlx** packages as part of atd 4.1.0 by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon): generic JSON-like AST and YAML-to-ATD bridge ([ocaml/opam-repository#29709](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29709))

weekly_summary.group_overview:
- __RUMINANT:core__ The **keep-expansion typechecker overhaul** [ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) merged after years of development by [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) and [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), resolving multiple non-termination and principality bugs ([ocaml/ocaml#9314](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/9314), [ocaml/ocaml#13230](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13230), [ocaml/ocaml#13881](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13881), [ocaml/ocaml#14036](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14036)); **primitive aliases** from [ocaml/RFCs#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44) landed in [ocaml/ocaml#13377](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13377), completing a full RFC-to-implementation cycle
- __RUMINANT:core__ Stdlib gained **`Atomic.update`** ([ocaml/ocaml#10798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10798)), **`Format.pp_print_iarray`** ([ocaml/ocaml#14724](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14724)), and **`Format.pp_print_dynarray`** ([ocaml/ocaml#14726](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14726)), bringing `iarray` and `Dynarray` closer to feature parity with `Array`; **ephemeron GC** fixes for 5.5 merged in [ocaml/ocaml#14718](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14718) and a structured multi-PR series ([ocaml/ocaml#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732)–[ocaml/ocaml#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735)) opened to harden lifecycle management
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) merged 30+ PRs spanning **Runtime 5 as the new default** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5780](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5780)), **custom `[@@or_null]` types** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5668](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5668)), **layout-poly `Texp_apply_layout` AST node** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5634](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5634)), **reversible name mangling** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5099](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5099)), Flambda2 reaper restructuring, and backend codegen improvements
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) completed its **OCaml 5.4 upgrade** ([oxcaml/merlin#227](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/227)), updated its magic number mapping to revision 36 ([oxcaml/merlin#231](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/231)), and extended compiler-flag test coverage ([oxcaml/merlin#232](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/232)); the **bootstrap compiler update to OCaml 5.4.1** in [oxcaml/opam-repository#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) remains open pending workflow resolution
- __RUMINANT:tools__ Three coordinated releases shipped: **dune 3.22.2** (reverts `--diff-command` behavior regression), **merlin v5.7.0-504** (experimental `refactor-extract-region` command), and **ocaml-lsp 1.26.0** (`refactorExtract`, `rangeFormatting`, build system agnosticism); **Wasm dynlink and toplevel** support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)) merged into js_of_ocaml after months of development
- __RUMINANT:tools__ Dune internals saw systematic library consolidation by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg): `csexp_rpc` merged into `rpc` ([ocaml/dune#14147](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14147)), `dune_rpc_client`/`dune_rpc_server` merged ([ocaml/dune#14145](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14145)), `fsevents` and `fswatch_win` merged into `dune_scheduler` ([ocaml/dune#14153](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14153), [ocaml/dune#14155](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14155))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) received the three major tooling releases plus 12+ new packages; notable additions include **ocaml-ai-sdk** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29679](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29679)), **yamlx** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29684](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29684)), and multimedia updates (**ffmpeg** 1.3.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29703](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29703)), **liquidsoap** 2.4.3 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29708](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29708)))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) added release changelogs for merlin and ocaml-lsp by [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3605)), the dune 3.22.1 announcement by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3596](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3596)), and two new platform tutorials on continuous benchmarking ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3598](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3598)) and OCaml-CI self-hosting ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3599](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3599)) by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac)
